    "R1.2": "The concluding paragraph infers that both the contrastive objective and the information-gain formulation are 'essential' from only two ablations. Checking whether that causal conclusion follows from the comparisons shown is a central logical task.",

Whether the evidence in the section actually licenses the SOTA claim is the single most critical alignment check.",
    "R1.4": "The SOTA and novelty claim depends on what the 'nearest prior work' is. No external systems are cited or compared, so assessing whether the delta is meaningful requires probing what is omitted."

rationale.R1.1: The section asserts a specific contribution claim (contrastive answer-prediction objective, KL-divergence scoring, information-gain motivation) and gap framing (beating prior work). Reconstructing the thesis is prerequisite to evaluating whether the framing is coherent.
rationale.R1.2: The concluding paragraph infers that both the contrastive objective and the information-gain formulation are 'essential' from only two ablations. Checking whether that causal conclusion follows from the comparisons shown is a central logical task.
rationale.R1.3: Load-bearing: the abstract and conclusion claim a 'new state of the art' over 'prior work', but the experimental table compares only two ablations of the same system — no external baseline appears. Whether the evidence in the section actually licenses the SOTA claim is the single most critical alignment check.
rationale.R1.4: The SOTA and novelty claim depends on what the 'nearest prior work' is. No external systems are cited or compared, so assessing whether the delta is meaningful requires probing what is omitted.

parsed.clarification_question: Should the review focus on technical content — checking whether claims (especially the SOTA assertion) are supported by the evidence presented and whether conclusions follow logically — or on prose quality and writing style?
